step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows two distinct flows: a batch processing pipeline and a real-time integration pipeline. In the batch pipeline, web users access a serverless Web UI protected by AWS WAF and hosted on CloudFront/S3, upload claims files to S3, which triggers a Step Functions workflow executing a Fargate container for ML analysis, saving results to RDS and notifying users via SES. In the real-time pipeline, external integration systems send API calls via API Gateway to a Lambda function running the same ML container, which stores results in RDS and returns predictions synchronously.

graph.name: IDA Technologies Fraud Waste Abuse SaaS
graph.notes: SaaS system designed to quickly identify Fraud Waste Abuse in health insurance claims using batch and real-time ML pipelines.

nodes.name: Batch User
nodes.notes: End user uploading claim files via web portal.

nodes.name: AWS WAF
nodes.notes: Secures the Web UI against web exploits.

nodes.name: AWS Fargate
nodes.notes: Runs the ML model in container tasks for batch analysis.

nodes.name: Integration Systems
nodes.notes: External client systems consuming real-time API.

nodes.name: Amazon API Gateway
nodes.notes: Exposes REST endpoints for real-time claims analysis.

nodes.name: AWS Lambda
nodes.notes: Runs containerized ML model for real-time execution.

nodes.name: Amazon RDS
nodes.notes: Relational database using dedicated schemas for multi-tenant isolation.

edges.notes: Spins up Fargate ML container task.
Sends response back to integration systems.
